runs.llm_reason: Out of position on a very connected broadway flop (AdQsJc) with deep stacks and no hero hole cards shown, checking is the safest legal default. The board heavily favors continuing ranges and contains many strong made hands and draws, so a blind flop stab risks running into resistance without visible hand-specific reasons to bet.

From BB out of position on a very high-connected broadway board (AdQsJc), checking is the most robust default without hole cards. This texture heavily favors many made hands and strong draws, so a blind small c-bet is less attractive OOP at deep SPR. Since no cards are known, avoiding an aggressive stab preserves flexibility against continued pressure.

Out of position on a very connected broadway flop (AdQsJc) with no visible hand information, checking is the safest default. The board heavily favors the in-position range and is dynamic enough that a blind small stab can be punished or called too wide. With deep SPR, preserving flexibility is preferable without a made-hand or draw-specific reason to bet.

Out of position on a highly connected Broadway flop (AdQsJc) with deep SPR, checking is the most flexible default without knowing hero’s hole cards. The board interacts strongly with both ranges, so bluff-c betting blind is risky and all-in is unnecessary.

Out of position on a very coordinated high-card flop (AdQsJc) with deep stacks and no hero hand information, checking is the lowest-risk default. This board interacts strongly with many in-position ranges, so a blind flop stab risks getting raised or called by many continuing hands. Without a specific value or draw, there is no clear reason to bet or shove in a deep-SPR spot.
